de.summary: MOOSEnger ist ein konversationeller KI-Agent für das MOOSE-Physik-Simulationsframework, das natürlichsprachliche Anfragen durch RAG und domänenbewusste Analyse in gültige Eingabespezifikationen umwandelt und eine Ausführungspass-Rate von 93% gegenüber 8% für nur LLM-Baselines erreicht. Das System kombiniert Dialoge mit deterministischer Validierung und iterativer Syntaxreparatur.

es.title: MOOSEnger -- Un Agente IA Específico del Dominio para el Ecosistema MOOSE
es.summary: MOOSEnger es un agente de IA conversacional para el marco de simulación de física MOOSE que convierte solicitudes en lenguaje natural a especificaciones de entrada válidas usando RAG y análisis consciente del dominio, logrando una tasa de aprobación de ejecución del 93% en comparación con el 8% para líneas de base solo LLM. El sistema combina diálogos con validación determinística y reparación sintáctica iterativa.

title: Breaking Contextual Inertia: Reinforcement Learning with Single-Turn Anchors for Stable Multi-Turn Interaction
summary: This paper identifies "Contextual Inertia," where LLMs fail to incorporate new information in multi-turn conversations, and proposes RLSTA, a training approach leveraging single-turn capabilities as anchors to stabilize multi-turn alignment. The method demonstrates strong cross-domain generalization without requiring external verifiers.
content:
arXiv:2603.04783v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While LLMs demonstrate strong reasoning capabilities when provided with full information in a single turn, they exhibit substantial vulnerability in multi-turn interactions. Specifically, when information is revealed incrementally or requires updates, models frequently fail to integrate new constraints, leading to a collapse in performance compared to their single-turn baselines. We term the root cause as \emph{Contextual Inertia}: a phenomenon where models rigidly adhere to previous reasoning traces. Even when users explicitly provide corrections or new data in later turns, the model ignores them, preferring to maintain consistency with its previous (incorrect) reasoning path. To address this, we introduce \textbf{R}einforcement \textbf{L}earning with \textbf{S}ingle-\textbf{T}urn \textbf{A}nchors (\textbf{RLSTA}), a generalizable training approach designed to stabilize multi-turn interaction across diverse scenarios and domains. RLSTA leverages the model's superior single-turn capabilities as stable internal anchors to provide reward signals. By aligning multi-turn responses with these anchors, RLSTA empowers models to break contextual inertia and self-calibrate their reasoning based on the latest information. Experiments show that RLSTA significantly outperforms standard fine-tuning and abstention-based methods. Notably, our method exhibits strong cross-domain generalization (e.g., math to code) and proves effective even without external verifiers, highlighting its potential for general-domain applications.

zh.title: 打破语境惯性：使用单轮锚点进行强化学习以实现稳定的多轮交互
zh.summary: 本文确定了"语境惯性"现象，其中大语言模型在多轮对话中无法整合新信息，并提出RLSTA，一种利用单轮能力作为锚点以稳定多轮对齐的训练方法。该方法在不需要外部验证者的情况下展示了强大的跨域泛化能力。

fr.title: Briser l'Inertie Contextuelle : Apprentissage par Renforcement avec Ancres Single-Turn pour une Interaction Multi-Turn Stable
fr.summary: Cet article identifie l'"Inertie Contextuelle", où les LLM échouent à incorporer les nouvelles informations dans les conversations multi-tours, et propose RLSTA, une approche d'entraînement exploitant les capacités single-turn comme ancres pour stabiliser l'alignement multi-tours. La méthode démontre une forte généralisation inter-domaines sans nécessiter de vérificateurs externes.

de.title: Kontextuelle Trägheit durchbrechen: Verstärktes Lernen mit Single-Turn-Ankern für stabile Multi-Turn-Interaktion
de.summary: Dieses Papier identifiziert "Kontextuelle Trägheit", bei der LLMs keine neuen Informationen in mehrteiligen Gesprächen einbeziehen, und schlägt RLSTA vor, einen Trainingsansatz, der Single-Turn-Fähigkeiten als Anker nutzt, um mehrteilige Ausrichtung zu stabilisieren. Die Methode zeigt starke domänenübergreifende Verallgemeinerung ohne externe Verifizierer.

es.title: Rompiendo la Inercia Contextual: Aprendizaje por Refuerzo con Anclas de Un Solo Turno para Interacción Multi-Turno Estable
es.summary: Este artículo identifica la "Inercia Contextual", donde los LLM no logran incorporar nueva información en conversaciones multi-turno, y propone RLSTA, un enfoque de entrenamiento que aprovecha capacidades de un solo turno como anclas para estabilizar el alineamiento multi-turno. El método demuestra una fuerte generalización entre dominios sin requerir verificadores externos.

title: Timer-S1: A Billion-Scale Time Series Foundation Model with Serial Scaling
summary: Timer-S1 is an 8.3B parameter mixture-of-experts time series model achieving state-of-the-art forecasting results through serial scaling of architecture, dataset (TimeBench with one trillion points), and training pipelines. The Serial-Token Prediction objective and post-training extensions enhance both short-term and long-context performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.04791v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce Timer-S1, a strong Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) time series foundation model with 8.3B total parameters, 0.75B activated parameters for each token, and a context length of 11.5K. To overcome the scalability bottleneck in existing pre-trained time series foundation models, we perform Serial Scaling in three dimensions: model architecture, dataset, and training pipeline. Timer-S1 integrates sparse TimeMoE blocks and generic TimeSTP blocks for Serial-Token Prediction (STP), a generic training objective that adheres to the serial nature of forecasting. The proposed paradigm introduces serial computations to improve long-term predictions while avoiding costly rolling-style inference and pronounced error accumulation in the standard next-token prediction. Pursuing a high-quality and unbiased training dataset, we curate TimeBench, a corpus with one trillion time points, and apply meticulous data augmentation to mitigate predictive bias. We further pioneer a post-training stage, including continued pre-training and long-context extension, to enhance short-term and long-context performance. Evaluated on the large-scale GIFT-Eval leaderboard, Timer-S1 achieves state-of-the-art forecasting performance, attaining the best MASE and CRPS scores as a pre-trained model. Timer-S1 will be released to facilitate further research.

zh.title: Timer-S1: 具有序列扩展的十亿规模时间序列基础模型
zh.summary: Timer-S1是一个83亿参数的混合专家时间序列模型，通过架构、数据集(TimeBench包含一万亿个数据点)和训练管道的序列扩展实现了最先进的预测结果。序列-令牌预测目标和后训练扩展增强了短期和长上下文性能。

fr.title: Timer-S1 : Un modèle de fondation de séries temporelles à l'échelle du milliard avec mise à l'échelle en série
fr.summary: Timer-S1 est un modèle de séries temporelles de 8,3 milliards de paramètres à mélange d'experts achievant des résultats de prévision dernier cri grâce à la mise à l'échelle en série de l'architecture, de l'ensemble de données (TimeBench avec un billion de points) et des pipelines d'entraînement. L'objectif de prédiction série-jeton et les extensions post-entraînement améliorent les performances à court terme et à long contexte.

de.title: Timer-S1: Ein billionenskaliges Zeitreihenfundamentalmodell mit serieller Skalierung
de.summary: Timer-S1 ist ein 8,3-Milliarden-Parameter-Mixture-of-Experts-Zeitreihenmodell, das durch serielle Skalierung der Architektur, des Datensatzes (TimeBench mit einer Billionen Punkten) und der Trainingspipelines hochmoderne Prognoseergebnisse erreicht. Das Serial-Token-Prediction-Ziel und Nachtrainingserweiterungen verbessern sowohl die kurzfristige als auch die langfristige Kontextleistung.

es.title: Timer-S1: Un modelo fundamental de series temporales a escala de mil millones con escalado en serie
es.summary: Timer-S1 es un modelo de series temporales de 8,3 mil millones de parámetros de mezcla de expertos que logra resultados de pronóstico de última generación mediante escalado en serie de la arquitectura, el conjunto de datos (TimeBench con un billón de puntos) y canalizaciones de capacitación. El objetivo de predicción serie-token y extensiones posteriores al entrenamiento mejoran el rendimiento de corto plazo y contexto largo.

title: EchoGuard: An Agentic Framework with Knowledge-Graph Memory for Detecting Manipulative Communication in Longitudinal Dialogue
summary: EchoGuard uses knowledge graphs as structured memory to detect manipulative communication patterns in longitudinal dialogue, combining episodic tracking of events and emotions with semantic pattern detection. The agentic framework generates targeted Socratic prompts grounded in detected manipulation to guide user self-awareness.
content:
arXiv:2603.04815v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Manipulative communication, such as gaslighting, guilt-tripping, and emotional coercion, is often difficult for individuals to recognize. Existing agentic AI systems lack the structured, longitudinal memory to track these subtle, context-dependent tactics, often failing due to limited context windows and catastrophic forgetting. We introduce EchoGuard, an agentic AI framework that addresses this gap by using a Knowledge Graph (KG) as the agent's core episodic and semantic memory. EchoGuard employs a structured Log-Analyze-Reflect loop: (1) users log interactions, which the agent structures as nodes and edges in a personal, episodic KG (capturing events, emotions, and speakers); (2) the system executes complex graph queries to detect six psychologically-grounded manipulation patterns (stored as a semantic KG); and (3) an LLM generates targeted Socratic prompts grounded by the subgraph of detected patterns, guiding users toward self-discovery. This framework demonstrates how the interplay between agentic architectures and Knowledge Graphs can empower individuals in recognizing manipulative communication while maintaining personal autonomy and safety. We present the theoretical foundation, framework design, a comprehensive evaluation strategy, and a vision to validate this approach.

zh.title: EchoGuard: 一个用于检测纵向对话中操纵性沟通的智能体框架与知识图谱记忆
zh.summary: EchoGuard使用知识图谱作为结构化记忆来检测纵向对话中的操纵性沟通模式，结合事件和情感的情节跟踪与语义模式检测。该智能体框架生成基于检测到的操纵的有针对性的苏格拉底式提示，以指导用户的自我意识。

fr.title: EchoGuard : Un cadre agentique avec mémoire de graphe de connaissances pour détecter la communication manipulatrice dans le dialogue longitudinal
fr.summary: EchoGuard utilise des graphes de connaissances comme mémoire structurée pour détecter les modèles de communication manipulatrice dans le dialogue longitudinal, combinant le suivi épisodique des événements et des émotions avec la détection de motifs sémantiques. Le cadre agentique génère des invites socratiques ciblées enracinées dans la manipulation détectée pour guider la conscience de soi de l'utilisateur.

de.title: EchoGuard: Ein agentenbasiertes Framework mit Wissensgraph-Gedächtnis zur Erkennung manipulativer Kommunikation im Langzeitdialog
de.summary: EchoGuard nutzt Wissensgraphen als strukturiertes Gedächtnis, um manipulative Kommunikationsmuster im Langzeitdialog zu erkennen und verbindet dabei episodisches Tracking von Ereignissen und Emotionen mit semantischer Mustererkennung. Das agentenbasierte Framework generiert zielgerichtete sokratische Fragen, die in erkannter Manipulation verankert sind, um das Selbstbewusstsein des Benutzers zu fördern.

es.title: EchoGuard: Un marco agencial con memoria de gráfico de conocimiento para detectar comunicación manipuladora en diálogos longitudinales
es.summary: EchoGuard utiliza gráficos de conocimiento como memoria estructurada para detectar patrones de comunicación manipuladora en diálogos longitudinales, combinando el seguimiento episódico de eventos y emociones con la detección de patrones semánticos. El marco agencial genera indicaciones socráticas dirigidas fundamentadas en la manipulación detectada para guiar la autoconciencia del usuario.

title: LLM-Grounded Explainability for Port Congestion Prediction via Temporal Graph Attention Networks
summary: AIS-TGNN couples temporal graph neural networks with LLM reasoning to predict port congestion while generating operationally interpretable explanations grounded in model evidence, achieving 0.761 AUC with 99.6% consistency. The framework demonstrates auditable explainability for maritime supply-chain risk management without sacrificing performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.04818v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Port congestion at major maritime hubs disrupts global supply chains, yet existing prediction systems typically prioritize forecasting accuracy without providing operationally interpretable explanations. This paper proposes AIS-TGNN, an evidence-grounded framework that jointly performs congestion-escalation prediction and faithful natural-language explanation by coupling a Temporal Graph Attention Network (TGAT) with a structured large language model (LLM) reasoning module. Daily spatial graphs are constructed from Automatic Identification System (AIS) broadcasts, where each grid cell represents localized vessel activity and inter-cell interactions are modeled through attention-based message passing. The TGAT predictor captures spatiotemporal congestion dynamics, while model-internal evidence, including feature z-scores and attention-derived neighbor influence, is transformed into structured prompts that constrain LLM reasoning to verifiable model outputs. To evaluate explanatory reliability, we introduce a directional-consistency validation protocol that quantitatively measures agreement between generated narratives and underlying statistical evidence. Experiments on six months of AIS data from the Port of Los Angeles and Long Beach demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms both LR and GCN baselines, achieving a test AUC of 0.761, AP of 0.344, and recall of 0.504 under a strict chronological split while producing explanations with 99.6% directional consistency. Results show that grounding LLM generation in graph-model evidence enables interpretable and auditable risk reporting without sacrificing predictive performance. The framework provides a practical pathway toward operationally deployable explainable AI for maritime congestion monitoring and supply-chain risk management.

zh.title: LLM基础的港口拥堵预测的可解释性：通过时间图注意网络
zh.summary: AIS-TGNN结合时间图神经网络与大语言模型推理来预测港口拥堵，同时生成基于模型证据的可操作可解释说明，实现0.761 AUC和99.6%一致性。该框架为海事供应链风险管理演示了可审计的可解释性，而不牺牲性能。

fr.title: Explicabilité fondée sur les LLM pour la prédiction de la congestion portuaire via les réseaux d'attention temporelle graphique
fr.summary: AIS-TGNN couple les réseaux de neurones graphiques temporels avec le raisonnement LLM pour prédire la congestion portuaire tout en générant des explications interprétables sur le plan opérationnel enracinées dans les preuves du modèle, réalisant 0,761 AUC avec 99,6% de cohérence. Le framework démontre l'explicabilité vérifiable pour la gestion des risques de la chaîne d'approvisionnement maritime sans sacrifier les performances.

de.title: LLM-gestützte Erklärbarkeit für Hafenüberlastungsprognose über zeitliche Graphenaufmerksamkeitsnetzwerke
de.summary: AIS-TGNN kombiniert zeitliche Graphenneuronale Netzwerke mit LLM-Reasoning, um Hafenüberlastung vorherzusagen und dabei operativ interpretierbare Erklärungen auf Grundlage von Modellevidenzen zu generieren, wobei 0,761 AUC mit 99,6%iger Konsistenz erreicht wird. Das Framework demonstriert nachvollziehbare Erklärbarkeit für das Risikomanagement der maritimen Lieferkette, ohne dabei die Leistung zu beeinträchtigen.

es.title: Explicabilidad fundamentada en LLM para predicción de congestión portuaria a través de redes de atención gráfica temporal
es.summary: AIS-TGNN acopla redes neuronales gráficas temporales con razonamiento LLM para predecir la congestión portuaria mientras genera explicaciones operacionalmente interpretables fundamentadas en la evidencia del modelo, logrando 0,761 AUC con 99,6% de consistencia. El framework demuestra explicabilidad auditable para la gestión de riesgos de la cadena de suministro marítima sin sacrificar el rendimiento.

title: VISA: Value Injection via Shielded Adaptation for Personalized LLM Alignment
summary: VISA addresses the alignment tax in LLM fine-tuning by training a value-rewriter with GRPO to simultaneously optimize fine-grained value alignment and preserve semantic integrity and factual knowledge. The closed-loop framework significantly outperforms standard fine-tuning in maintaining personalized values while avoiding hallucinations.
content:
arXiv:2603.04822v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with nuanced human values remains a critical challenge, as existing methods like Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) often handle only coarse-grained attributes. In practice, fine-tuning LLMs on task-specific datasets to optimize value alignment inevitably incurs an alignment tax: the model's pre-calibrated value system drifts significantly due to latent bias absorption from training data, while the fine-tuning process also causes severe hallucinations and semantic information loss in generated responses. To address this, we propose VISA (Value Injection via Shielded Adaptation), a closed-loop framework designed to navigate this trade-off. VISA's architecture features a high-precision value detector, a semantic-to-value translator, and a core value-rewriter. The value-rewriter is trained via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with a composite reward function that simultaneously optimizes for fine-grained value precision, and the preservation of semantic integrity. By learning an optimal policy to balance these competing objectives, VISA effectively mitigates the alignment tax while staying loyal to the original knowledge. Our experiments demonstrate that this approach enables precise control over a model's value expression while maintaining its factual consistency and general capabilities, significantly outperforming both standard fine-tuning methods and prompting-based baselines, including GPT-4o.

zh.title: VISA：通过屏蔽自适应进行价值注入以实现个性化LLM对齐
zh.summary: VISA通过使用GRPO训练价值重写器，同时优化细粒度价值对齐并保持语义完整性和事实知识，来解决LLM微调中的对齐税问题。这个闭环框架在保持个性化价值的同时避免幻觉，显著超越了标准微调的性能。

fr.title: VISA : Injection de valeurs via adaptation protégée pour l'alignement personnalisé des LLM
fr.summary: VISA résout le coût d'alignement dans le fine-tuning des LLM en entraînant un ré-écrivain de valeurs avec GRPO pour optimiser simultanément l'alignement de valeurs à grain fin et préserver l'intégrité sémantique et les connaissances factuelles. Le cadre en boucle fermée surpasse significativement le fine-tuning standard pour maintenir les valeurs personnalisées tout en évitant les hallucinations.

de.title: VISA: Wert-Injektion durch geschützte Anpassung für personalisierte LLM-Ausrichtung
de.summary: VISA adressiert die Ausrichtungssteuer beim LLM-Fine-Tuning durch Training eines Wert-Umschreibers mit GRPO, um gleichzeitig die feinkörnige Wertausrichtung zu optimieren und semantische Integrität sowie faktisches Wissen zu bewahren. Das Closed-Loop-Framework übertrifft standardmäßiges Fine-Tuning beim Beibehalten personalisierter Werte und verhindert Halluzinationen.

es.title: VISA: Inyección de valores mediante adaptación protegida para alineación personalizada de LLM
es.summary: VISA aborda el impuesto de alineación en el fine-tuning de LLM entrenando un reescritor de valores con GRPO para optimizar simultáneamente la alineación de valores de grano fino y preservar la integridad semántica y el conocimiento fáctico. El marco de ciclo cerrado supera significativamente el fine-tuning estándar en el mantenimiento de valores personalizados mientras evita alucinaciones.

title: Design Behaviour Codes (DBCs): A Taxonomy-Driven Layered Governance Benchmark for Large Language Models
summary: Design Behaviour Codes introduce a system prompt governance layer with 150 controls that reduces LLM risk exposure by 36.8% compared to baseline, evaluated across 30 risk domains using adversarial red-teaming. The model-agnostic framework provides auditable governance compatible with EU AI Act requirements without training-time modifications.
content:
arXiv:2603.04837v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce the Dynamic Behavioral Constraint (DBC) benchmark, the first empirical framework for evaluating the efficacy of a structured, 150-control behavioral governance layer, the MDBC (Madan DBC) system, applied at inference time to large language models (LLMs). Unlike training time alignment methods (RLHF, DPO) or post-hoc content moderation APIs, DBCs constitute a system prompt level governance layer that is model-agnostic, jurisdiction-mappable, and auditable. We evaluate the DBC Framework across a 30 domain risk taxonomy organized into six clusters (Hallucination and Calibration, Bias and Fairness, Malicious Use, Privacy and Data Protection, Robustness and Reliability, and Misalignment Agency) using an agentic red-team protocol with five adversarial attack strategies (Direct, Roleplay, Few-Shot, Hypothetical, Authority Spoof) across 3 model families. Our three-arm controlled design (Base, Base plus Moderation, Base plus DBC) enables causal attribution of risk reduction. Key findings: the DBC layer reduces the aggregate Risk Exposure Rate (RER) from 7.19 percent (Base) to 4.55 percent (Base plus DBC), representing a 36.8 percent relative risk reduction, compared with 0.6 percent for a standard safety moderation prompt. MDBC Adherence Scores improve from 8.6 by 10 (Base) to 8.7 by 10 (Base plus DBC). EU AI Act compliance (automated scoring) reaches 8.5by 10 under the DBC layer. A three judge evaluation ensemble yields Fleiss kappa greater than 0.70 (substantial agreement), validating our automated pipeline. Cluster ablation identifies the Integrity Protection cluster (MDBC 081 099) as delivering the highest per domain risk reduction, while graybox adversarial attacks achieve a DBC Bypass Rate of 4.83 percent . We release the benchmark code, prompt database, and all evaluation artefacts to enable reproducibility and longitudinal tracking as models evolve.

zh.title: 设计行为代码（DBC）：用于大型语言模型的分类驱动的分层治理基准
zh.summary: 设计行为代码引入了一个系统提示治理层，包含150个控制措施，在30个风险域中通过对抗性红队测试评估，相比基线降低LLM风险暴露度36.8%。这个与模型无关的框架提供可审计的治理，兼容欧盟AI法案要求，无需训练时间修改。

fr.title: Codes de Comportement de Conception (DBC) : Un benchmark de gouvernance hiérarchisée guidé par la taxonomie pour les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Les codes de comportement de conception introduisent une couche de gouvernance des invites système avec 150 contrôles qui réduisent l'exposition aux risques des LLM de 36,8% par rapport à la ligne de base, évaluée dans 30 domaines de risque en utilisant des tests d'équipe adverse. Le cadre indépendant du modèle fournit une gouvernance vérifiable compatible avec les exigences de la Loi sur l'IA de l'UE sans modifications au moment de l'entraînement.

de.title: Design Behavior Codes (DBCs): Ein taxonomiegestütztes geschichtetes Governance-Benchmark für große Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Design Behavior Codes führen eine Systemanleitung-Governance-Schicht mit 150 Kontrollen ein, die die Risikoexposition von LLMs um 36,8% gegenüber der Baseline reduziert, bewertet über 30 Risikobereiche mit gegnerischem Red-Teaming. Das modellunabhängige Framework bietet nachvollziehbare Governance, die mit den Anforderungen des EU AI Act kompatibel ist, ohne Änderungen zur Trainingszeit.

es.title: Códigos de Comportamiento de Diseño (DBC): Un benchmark de gobernanza estratificada impulsada por taxonomía para grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: Los Códigos de Comportamiento de Diseño introducen una capa de gobernanza de indicación de sistema con 150 controles que reducen la exposición al riesgo de LLM en un 36,8% en comparación con la línea de base, evaluada en 30 dominios de riesgo mediante pruebas de equipo rojo adversarial. El marco agnóstico del modelo proporciona gobernanza auditable compatible con los requisitos de la Ley de IA de la UE sin modificaciones en tiempo de entrenamiento.

title: On Multi-Step Theorem Prediction via Non-Parametric Structural Priors
summary: This paper proposes Theorem Precedence Graphs encoding structural dependencies from reasoning traces to enable LLMs to perform multi-step theorem prediction with 89.29% accuracy without gradient optimization. The approach addresses "Structural Drift," where standard in-context learning performance degrades sharply with increasing reasoning depth.
content:
arXiv:2603.04852v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-step theorem prediction is a central challenge in automated reasoning. Existing neural-symbolic approaches rely heavily on supervised parametric models, which exhibit limited generalization to evolving theorem libraries. In this work, we explore training-free theorem prediction through the lens of in-context learning (ICL). We identify a critical scalability bottleneck, termed Structural Drift: as reasoning depth increases, the performance of vanilla ICL degrades sharply, often collapsing to near zero. We attribute this failure to the LLM's inability to recover latent topological dependencies, leading to unstructured exploration. To address this issue, we propose Theorem Precedence Graphs, which encode temporal dependencies from historical solution traces as directed graphs, and impose explicit topological constraints that effectively prune the search space during inference. Coupled with retrieval-augmented graph construction and a stepwise symbolic executor, our approach enables LLMs to act as structured planners without any gradient-based optimization. Experiments on the FormalGeo7k benchmark show that our method achieves 89.29% accuracy, substantially outperforming ICL baselines and matching state-of-the-art supervised models. These results indicate that explicit structural priors offer a promising direction for scaling LLM-based symbolic reasoning.

zh.title: 通过非参数结构先验的多步定理预测
zh.summary: 本文提出定理先导图，从推理轨迹中编码结构依赖关系，使LLM能够以89.29%的精度执行多步定理预测，无需梯度优化。该方法解决了"结构漂移"问题，其中标准上下文学习的性能随着推理深度的增加而急剧下降。

fr.title: Sur la prédiction de théorèmes multi-étapes via des priors structurels non-paramétriques
fr.summary: Cet article propose des graphes de précédence de théorèmes encodant les dépendances structurelles des traces de raisonnement pour permettre aux LLM de réaliser la prédiction de théorèmes multi-étapes avec une précision de 89,29% sans optimisation par gradient. L'approche aborde la « dérive structurelle », où les performances d'apprentissage en contexte standard se dégradent considérablement avec une profondeur de raisonnement croissante.

de.title: Über die mehrstufige Theoremvorhersage via nicht-parametrische strukturelle Priors
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt Theorem-Präzedenzgraphen vor, die strukturelle Abhängigkeiten aus Begründungsspuren kodieren, um LLMs die mehrstufige Theoremvorhersage mit 89,29% Genauigkeit ohne Gradientenoptimierung zu ermöglichen. Der Ansatz adressiert „Strukturelle Drift", bei der die Leistung des standardmäßigen In-Context-Lernens mit zunehmender Begründungstiefe stark abnimmt.

es.title: Sobre la predicción de teoremas de múltiples pasos mediante antecedentes estructurales no paramétricos
es.summary: Este artículo propone Gráficos de Precedencia de Teoremas que codifican dependencias estructurales de trazas de razonamiento para permitir que los LLM realicen predicción de teoremas de múltiples pasos con una precisión del 89,29% sin optimización de gradientes. El enfoque aborda la "Deriva Estructural", donde el desempeño del aprendizaje en contexto estándar se degrada significativamente con una profundidad de razonamiento creciente.

title: Causally Robust Reward Learning from Reason-Augmented Preference Feedback
summary: ReCouPLe improves reward learning from preference feedback by using natural language rationales as causal signals, enabling models to identify genuine preference drivers rather than spurious correlations. The framework achieves 1.5-2x robustness improvements under distribution shift and transfers preference knowledge across tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.04861v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Preference-based reward learning is widely used for shaping agent behavior to match a user's preference, yet its sparse binary feedback makes it especially vulnerable to causal confusion. The learned reward often latches onto spurious features that merely co-occur with preferred trajectories during training, collapsing when those correlations disappear or reverse at test time. We introduce ReCouPLe, a lightweight framework that uses natural language rationales to provide the missing causal signal. Each rationale is treated as a guiding projection axis in an embedding space, training the model to score trajectories based on features aligned with that axis while de-emphasizing context that is unrelated to the stated reason. Because the same rationales (e.g., "avoids collisions", "completes the task faster") can appear across multiple tasks, ReCouPLe naturally reuses the same causal direction whenever tasks share semantics, and transfers preference knowledge to novel tasks without extra data or language-model fine-tuning. Our learned reward model can ground preferences on the articulated reason, aligning better with user intent and generalizing beyond spurious features. ReCouPLe outperforms baselines by up to 1.5x in reward accuracy under distribution shifts, and 2x in downstream policy performance in novel tasks. We have released our code at https://github.com/mj-hwang/ReCouPLe

zh.title: 因果鲁棒奖励学习：从理由增强的偏好反馈出发
zh.summary: ReCouPLe通过使用自然语言理由作为因果信号，改进了从偏好反馈中的奖励学习，使模型能够识别真正的偏好驱动因素而非虚假相关性。该框架在分布偏移下实现了1.5-2倍的鲁棒性改进，并可跨任务转移偏好知识。

fr.title: Apprentissage des récompenses robuste aux causes à partir de commentaires de préférences augmentés par des raisons
fr.summary: ReCouPLe améliore l'apprentissage des récompenses à partir de commentaires de préférences en utilisant des justifications en langage naturel comme signaux causaux, permettant aux modèles d'identifier les vrais moteurs de préférence plutôt que les corrélations fallacieuses. Le cadre réalise des améliorations de robustesse de 1,5-2x en cas de changement de distribution et transfère les connaissances de préférence entre les tâches.

de.title: Kausal robustes Belohnungslernen aus begründungsaugmentiertem Präferenzfeedback
de.summary: ReCouPLe verbessert das Belohnungslernen aus Präferenzfeedback durch die Verwendung natürlichsprachlicher Begründungen als kausale Signale, wodurch Modelle echte Präferenztreiber statt spurioser Korrelationen erkennen können. Das Framework erreicht 1,5-2x Robustheitsverbesserungen unter Verteilungsverschiebung und überträgt Präferenzwissen zwischen Aufgaben.

es.title: Aprendizaje de recompensas causalmente robusto a partir de comentarios de preferencia aumentados con razones
es.summary: ReCouPLe mejora el aprendizaje de recompensas a partir de comentarios de preferencia utilizando justificaciones en lenguaje natural como señales causales, permitiendo que los modelos identifiquen los verdaderos impulsores de preferencia en lugar de correlaciones espurias. El marco logra mejoras de robustez de 1,5-2x bajo cambios de distribución y transfiere conocimiento de preferencia entre tareas.

title: K-Gen: A Multimodal Language-Conditioned Approach for Interpretable Keypoint-Guided Trajectory Generation
summary: K-Gen generates interpretable trajectory keypoints by reasoning over rasterized BEV maps and scene descriptions using multimodal LLMs, then refines them into full trajectories, outperforming baselines on WOMD and nuPlan. The approach combines multimodal reasoning with trajectory-aware reinforcement fine-tuning for autonomous driving simulation.
content:
arXiv:2603.04868v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generating realistic and diverse trajectories is a critical challenge in autonomous driving simulation. While Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise, existing methods often rely on structured data like vectorized maps, which fail to capture the rich, unstructured visual context of a scene. To address this, we propose K-Gen, an interpretable keypoint-guided multimodal framework that leverages Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to unify rasterized BEV map inputs with textual scene descriptions. Instead of directly predicting full trajectories, K-Gen generates interpretable keypoints along with reasoning that reflects agent intentions, which are subsequently refined into accurate trajectories by a refinement module. To further enhance keypoint generation, we apply T-DAPO, a trajectory-aware reinforcement fine-tuning algorithm. Experiments on WOMD and nuPlan demonstrate that K-Gen outperforms existing baselines, highlighting the effectiveness of combining multimodal reasoning with keypoint-guided trajectory generation.

zh.title: K-Gen：用于可解释关键点引导轨迹生成的多模态语言条件方法
zh.summary: K-Gen通过对栅栏化BEV地图和场景描述进行推理，使用多模态大语言模型生成可解释的轨迹关键点，然后将其细化为完整轨迹，在WOMD和nuPlan上优于基准方法。该方法将多模态推理与轨迹感知强化微调相结合，用于自主驾驶仿真。

fr.title: K-Gen : Une approche multimodale et conditionnée par le langage pour la génération de trajectoires guidées par des points clés interprétables
fr.summary: K-Gen génère des points clés de trajectoire interprétables en raisonnant sur des cartes BEV rastérisées et des descriptions de scènes à l'aide de LLM multimodaux, puis les affine en trajectoires complètes, surpassant les lignes de base sur WOMD et nuPlan. L'approche combine le raisonnement multimodal avec l'affinage par renforcement conscient de la trajectoire pour la simulation de conduite autonome.

de.title: K-Gen: Ein multimodaler sprachgesteuerter Ansatz zur interpretierbaren schlüsselpunktgesteuerten Trajektorienerzeugung
de.summary: K-Gen generiert interpretierbare Trajektorienschlüsselpunkte durch Argumentation über rastergescannte BEV-Karten und Szenenbeschreibungen mit multimodalen LLMs und verfeinert sie dann zu vollständigen Trajektorien, wobei es Baselines auf WOMD und nuPlan übertrifft. Der Ansatz kombiniert multimodales Denken mit trajektoriengerechtem Reinforcement Fine-Tuning zur autonomen Fahrsimulation.

es.title: K-Gen: Un enfoque multimodal condicionado por lenguaje para la generación interpretable de trayectorias guiadas por puntos clave
es.summary: K-Gen genera puntos clave de trayectoria interpretables razonando sobre mapas BEV rasterizados y descripciones de escenas usando LLM multimodales, luego los refina en trayectorias completas, superando los modelos base en WOMD y nuPlan. El enfoque combina razonamiento multimodal con afinamiento de refuerzo consciente de trayectorias para la simulación de conducción autónoma.

title: SEA-TS: Self-Evolving Agent for Autonomous Code Generation of Time Series Forecasting Algorithms
summary: SEA-TS autonomously generates optimized time series forecasting code through iterative evolution combining Monte Carlo tree search with code review and architecture search, achieving 40% MAE reduction over TimeMixer. The system discovers novel patterns like physics-informed constraints, demonstrating that autonomous ML engineering can innovate beyond manual design.
content:
arXiv:2603.04873v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Accurate time series forecasting underpins decision-making across domains, yet conventional ML development suffers from data scarcity in new deployments, poor adaptability under distribution shift, and diminishing returns from manual iteration. We propose Self-Evolving Agent for Time Series Algorithms (SEA-TS), a framework that autonomously generates, validates, and optimizes forecasting code via an iterative self-evolution loop. Our framework introduces three key innovations: (1) Metric-Advantage Monte Carlo Tree Search (MA-MCTS), which replaces fixed rewards with a normalized advantage score for discriminative search guidance; (2) Code Review with running prompt refinement, where each executed solution undergoes automated review followed by prompt updates that encode corrective patterns, preventing recurrence of similar errors; and (3) Global Steerable Reasoning, which compares each node against global best and worst solutions, enabling cross-trajectory knowledge transfer. We adopt a MAP-Elites archive for architectural diversity. On the public Solar-Energy benchmark, SEA-TS generated code achieves a 40% MAE reduction relative to TimeMixer, surpassing state-of-the-art methods. On proprietary datasets, SEA-TS generated code reduces WAPE by 8.6% on solar PV forecasting and 7.7% on residential load forecasting compared to human-engineered baselines, and achieves 26.17% MAPE on load forecasting versus 29.34% by TimeMixer. Notably, the evolved models discover novel architectural patterns--including physics-informed monotonic decay heads encoding solar irradiance constraints, per-station learned diurnal cycle profiles, and learnable hourly bias correction--demonstrating that autonomous ML engineering can generate genuinely novel algorithmic ideas beyond manual design.

zh.title: SEA-TS：用于时间序列预测算法自主代码生成的自我进化代理
zh.summary: SEA-TS通过将蒙特卡洛树搜索与代码审查和架构搜索相结合的迭代进化，自主生成优化的时间序列预测代码，与TimeMixer相比MAE减少40%。该系统发现了物理启发约束等新颖模式，证明自主机器学习工程可以超越手工设计进行创新。

fr.title: SEA-TS : Agent auto-évolutif pour la génération autonome de code d'algorithmes de prévision de séries temporelles
fr.summary: SEA-TS génère automatiquement du code de prévision de séries temporelles optimisé par évolution itérative combinant la recherche par arbre de Monte Carlo avec l'examen du code et la recherche d'architecture, réalisant une réduction MAE de 40% par rapport à TimeMixer. Le système découvre des modèles novateurs comme les contraintes informées par la physique, démontrant que l'ingénierie ML autonome peut innover au-delà de la conception manuelle.

de.title: SEA-TS: Selbst-evolvierender Agent für autonome Codegenerierung von Zeitreihenprognose-Algorithmen
de.summary: SEA-TS generiert autonom optimierten Zeitreihenprognose-Code durch iterative Entwicklung, die Monte-Carlo-Baumsuche mit Code-Überprüfung und Architektursuche kombiniert und eine 40%ige MAE-Reduktion gegenüber TimeMixer erreicht. Das System entdeckt neuartige Muster wie physikgestützte Beschränkungen und zeigt, dass autonomes ML-Engineering über manuelle Entwürfe hinaus innovativ sein kann.

es.title: SEA-TS: Agente auto-evolutivo para la generación autónoma de código de algoritmos de pronóstico de series temporales
es.summary: SEA-TS genera automáticamente código optimizado de pronóstico de series temporales mediante evolución iterativa que combina búsqueda de árbol de Monte Carlo con revisión de código y búsqueda de arquitectura, logrando una reducción del 40% en MAE en comparación con TimeMixer. El sistema descubre patrones novedosos como restricciones informadas por la física, demostrando que la ingeniería de ML autónoma puede innovar más allá del diseño manual.
